title: The Reset Breath
noraOpener: Every great athlete makes mistakes. What separates champions is how fast they reset. Today you learn the fastest reset there is: one strong breath.
noraProbe: Think of the last time a mistake stuck with you during a game. What did it feel like?
probeChoices: Stuck in my head about it
takeawayCue: One breath, then next play.

title: Talk Like a Champion
noraOpener: You talk to yourself more than anyone else talks to you. Today you practice making that voice a coach, not a critic.
noraProbe: What does the voice in your head usually say after a rough play?
takeawayCue: Talk to yourself like you'd talk to a teammate.

title: Shake It Off
noraOpener: Mistakes are heavy only if you carry them. Today you practice putting one down and moving to the next play lighter.
noraProbe: When a play goes wrong, how long does it usually stay in your head?
takeawayCue: Flush it, then move.

title: Your Effort Story
noraOpener: Scoreboards change. Effort is yours to keep. Today you practice being proud of the work, win or lose.
noraProbe: What is one thing you did this week that took real effort, even if nobody noticed?
takeawayCue: Effort is something to be proud of every day.

title: Checkpoint: Bounce-Back Basics
noraOpener: You finished Bounce-Back Basics. Before we move on, let's lock in what you learned: the reset breath, coach-voice self-talk, shaking off mistakes, and owning your effort.
noraProbe: Which bounce-back skill helped you most this unit, and when did you actually use it?
takeawayCue: You know how to bounce back. Use it every practice.

title: Your Inner Coach
noraOpener: Every athlete has two voices inside: a coach and a critic. Today you practice turning up the coach so it's the louder one when it counts.
noraProbe: If your inner coach could say one sentence to you before every game, what should it be?
takeawayCue: Let the coach talk, not the critic.

title: The Highlight Reel
noraOpener: Your brain replays what you feed it. Today you build a highlight reel of your best moments so confidence has proof to stand on.
noraProbe: What is one play or moment you're most proud of? Picture it in as much detail as you can.
probeChoices: A time I didn't give up
takeawayCue: Replay your best, not your worst.

title: Power Words
noraOpener: Champions carry short, strong words they can grab in a big moment. Today you pick yours and practice using them.
noraProbe: What word or short phrase makes you feel strong when you say it?
takeawayCue: One power word, ready when you need it.

title: Proof You Can
noraOpener: Confidence isn't pretending. It's remembering. Today you collect real proof of hard things you've already done, so doubt has less room to argue.
noraProbe: What is one hard thing you couldn't do a year ago that you can do now?
probeChoices: A skill I couldn't do before
takeawayCue: You've done hard things before. That's your proof.

title: Checkpoint: Confident Self-Talk
noraOpener: You finished Confident Self-Talk. You built an inner coach, a highlight reel, power words, and real proof you can do hard things.
noraProbe: What does your self-talk sound like now compared to when you started this unit?
takeawayCue: Your voice is your corner. Keep it on your side.

title: Find Your Focus Point
noraOpener: Focus is like a flashlight. It can only shine on one thing at a time. Today you learn to point it where you want.
noraProbe: During a game, where does your attention wander when things get slow?
takeawayCue: Pick one point, come back to it.

title: Tunnel Vision
noraOpener: Crowds get loud. Teammates chirp. Today you practice keeping your attention on the signal, not the noise.
noraProbe: What is the biggest distraction for you during competition?
takeawayCue: Signal over noise.

title: Distraction Drill
noraOpener: You can't stop distractions from showing up. You can practice noticing them and coming back fast. That's the whole skill.
noraProbe: When you lose focus, how do you usually notice it happened?
takeawayCue: Notice, then come back. Every return makes you stronger.

title: Pre-Game Reset
noraOpener: The minutes before a game decide how you start it. Today you build a short routine that gets your mind ready on purpose.
noraProbe: What do you usually do in the last five minutes before competition starts?
takeawayCue: Same routine, every game.

title: Checkpoint: Finding Focus
noraOpener: You finished Finding Focus. You learned to pick a focus point, cut through noise, come back from distractions, and reset before games.
noraProbe: When did you catch yourself losing focus this week, and what did you do about it?
takeawayCue: Focus is a skill you now know how to train.

title: Your Pressure Routine
noraOpener: Pressure moments feel huge because they're new every time. A routine makes them familiar. Today you build yours: breath, cue, go.
noraProbe: What is the highest-pressure moment in your sport: a free throw, a penalty, a serve, a start?
probeChoices: The last minutes of a close game
takeawayCue: Breath, cue, go. Same routine every time.

title: Slow the Moment
noraOpener: When pressure hits, everything speeds up: your heart, your thoughts, your feet. Today you practice the skill of slowing one thing down so the rest can follow.
noraProbe: What speeds up first for you under pressure: your heart, your thoughts, or your body?
takeawayCue: Slow one thing down and the rest follows.

title: Picture the Play
noraOpener: Your brain can practice without your body moving. Today you run the play in your mind first, so the real moment feels like the second time.
noraProbe: What play or move do you want to picture going perfectly?
takeawayCue: See it first, then do it.

title: Lock In on Command
noraOpener: You've built the pieces: a routine, a slow breath, a picture in your mind. Today you put them together and practice locking in on command.
noraProbe: What is your signal to lock in: stepping on the field, chalking up, tying your laces?
probeChoices: Stepping onto the field or court
takeawayCue: You can lock in on purpose. That's your edge.

title: Checkpoint: Pressure Routines
noraOpener: You finished Pressure Routines. You built a routine, learned to slow the moment, pictured plays before they happen, and practiced locking in on command.
noraProbe: Describe your pressure routine in your own words. What are your steps?
takeawayCue: Pressure is familiar now. You have a routine for it.

title: Name That Feeling
noraOpener: Feelings are information, not instructions. Today you practice the first skill: giving the feeling a name so it gets smaller.
noraProbe: What feeling shows up most for you on game day: nerves, excitement, frustration, or something else?
takeawayCue: Name it to tame it.

title: Calm Your Body
noraOpener: Big feelings live in your body: fast heart, tight shoulders, quick breath. Today you learn to slow your body so your mind can follow.
noraProbe: Where do you feel nerves in your body first?
takeawayCue: Slow body, clear mind.

title: The Wave
noraOpener: Every feeling rises, peaks, and passes, like a wave. Today you practice riding one instead of fighting it.
noraProbe: Think of a strong feeling from this week. How long did it actually last?
probeChoices: It came and went in waves
takeawayCue: Feelings pass. Ride the wave.

title: Your Next Move
noraOpener: You can't always pick your feeling, but you can always pick your next move. Today you practice choosing one helpful action, and remember: asking for help counts as a strong move.
noraProbe: Who is one person you could talk to when a feeling gets too big to handle alone?
takeawayCue: Feel it, then choose your next move. Asking for help is a strong one.

title: Checkpoint: Name the Feeling
noraOpener: You finished Name the Feeling. You can name what you feel, calm your body, ride the wave, and choose your next move, including asking for help.
noraProbe: What feeling did you get better at handling this unit?
takeawayCue: Feelings are information. You know how to read them now.

title: The Long Exhale
noraOpener: Your breath is a remote control for your body. A long, slow exhale tells your whole system it's safe to settle. Today you practice using it.
noraProbe: When during a game or practice would a calmer body help you most?
takeawayCue: Long exhale, calm body.

title: Loosen Up
noraOpener: Stress hides in your body: tight shoulders, clenched jaw, gripped hands. Today you learn to find it and let it go, one spot at a time.
noraProbe: Where does your body hold tension when you're nervous or frustrated?
takeawayCue: Find the tight spot, breathe, let it go.

title: Cool Down the Fire
noraOpener: Frustration is fuel with no steering. Today you practice cooling it down fast, so you keep the energy without losing control.
noraProbe: What usually lights the fire for you: a bad call, a mistake, an opponent talking?
takeawayCue: Keep the fire, take the wheel.

title: Reset and Return
noraOpener: Calming down isn't the finish line. Getting back in the game is. Today you practice the full loop: notice, calm, return.
noraProbe: After a big feeling passes, what helps you get your head back in the game?
probeChoices: Moving on to the next play
takeawayCue: Notice, calm, return. The full loop.

title: Checkpoint: Calm Your Body
noraOpener: You finished Calm Your Body. You can use the long exhale, release tension, cool down frustration, and return to the game after a big feeling.
noraProbe: Which calming tool feels most like yours now, and when will you use it next?
takeawayCue: A calm body is a skill you carry everywhere.
